file_name: LECLANCHÉ S.A. - JOINT DEVELOPMENT AND MARKETING AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: The term "Affiliate" as used herein with respect to a party means any partnership, joint venture, corporation of other form of enterprise that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by or is under common control with such entity or person. For purposes hereof, "control" means the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of a person or entity, whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Affiliate" as used herein with respect to a party means any partnership, joint venture, corporation of other form of enterprise that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by or is under common control with such entity or person.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'control' within the 'Affiliate' definition has been removed. This creates an in-text contradiction because, while the term 'Affiliate' is still used throughout the contract (e.g., in the Standstill clause), the mechanism for determining 'control,' which is essential to determining affiliation, is absent. This affects the interpretation and enforcement of clauses that depend on accurately defining affiliations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge that both companies are publicly quoted companies and may before and/or during the course of this Agreement be in receipt of nonpublic information (including any Confidential Information) (together, "Non Public Information") and agree to comply with all relevant laws and regulations applicable to market trading during the term of this Agreement and for a period of 12 months thereafter, and in particular agrees not to: directly nor indirectly engage in any transactions on any exchange, regulated market or over-the-counter in (i) any securities issued by the Leclanché or any affiliated company or (ii) any derivative products the price of which is derived in whole or in substantial part from any such securities; divulge directly or indirectly any Non Public Information to any third party without the written consent of Leclanché and if it is authorized to make such disclosure, shall impose standstill obligations on that third party consistent with this provision.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Affiliate' and clarified what 'control' means in determining affiliation. The changed text removes the definition of 'control'. The contradicted text uses 'affiliated company,' relying on the original definition of 'Affiliate,' which included the definition of 'control.' Without the definition of control, it's now unclear which entities fall under 'affiliated company' within the standstill provision, creating a contradiction as the scope and enforceability of the standstill clause becomes ambiguous.

perturbation.original_text: The Products offered by Leclanché are set out in Annex I. Pricing will be determined after Phase 1 is completed.
perturbation.changed_text: The Products offered by Leclanché are set out in Annex I.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the sentence 'Pricing will be determined after Phase 1 is completed' creates an in-text contradiction. Section 3.1 now states that products are defined in Annex I, but fails to mention how the pricing of those items will be determined. This omission creates a contradiction as Section 7.1 references pricing being defined after Phase 1, which according to Section 3.1 no longer exists.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pricing for standard Products shall be defined after Phase 1 has been completed. Pricing for more complex projects shall be determined on a case by case basis. Pricing for Services shall be quoted on a case by case basis depending on the customer specification.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that pricing would be determined after Phase 1. The changed text removes this statement. The contradicted text states that pricing for standard products shall be defined after Phase 1. By removing the reference to Phase 1 in the changed text, a contradiction is created because the contradicted text now references a pricing determination mechanism (after Phase 1) that is not supported or defined in the changed text, making the pricing terms unenforceable and inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: Where Oak Ridge has identified a specific market opportunity which has been qualified together with Leclanché pursuant to 5.3 above, the Parties shall undertake to work exclusively with each other on such opportunities;
perturbation.changed_text: Where Oak Ridge has identified a specific market opportunity which has been qualified together with Leclanché, the Parties shall undertake to work exclusively with each other on such opportunities;
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference 'pursuant to 5.3 above', there is an in-text contradiction as to how market opportunities should be qualified. This creates uncertainty as to what steps need to be taken when qualifiying specific market opportunities, potentially leading to disputes over exclusivity rights, since this is mentioned in Section 5.3
perturbation.contradicted_text: Oak Ridge shall identify commercial opportunities and work with Leclanché to qualify these opportunities and prepare bids and quotations as necessary;
perturbation.justification: The original text specified qualification of market opportunities 'pursuant to 5.3 above,' providing a clear method. The changed text removes this reference, creating ambiguity about how market opportunities should be qualified, directly contradicting the requirement in the contradicted text that 'Oak Ridge shall identify commercial opportunities and work with Leclanché to qualify these opportunities'. This creates uncertainty and a potential conflict regarding the agreed-upon procedure for qualification, impacting obligations and enforcement.
